name: Novelda - La Mola (Circular PR-CV 311)
region_intro: Hiking around Novelda offers diverse landscapes within Spain's Alicante province, primarily centered in the Vinalopó Valley. The terrain features rolling hills, olive groves, and vineyards, alongside unique natural formations like the terracotta canyon of Les Salinetes de Novelda. Higher elevations include hills such as La Mola, providing panoramic views of the town and surrounding areas. The region combines natural exploration with access to cultural landmarks. Best hiking trails around Novelda The most popular hiking route is Novelda - La Mola (Circular PR-CV 311), a 6.8 miles (11.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes an ascent to La Mola, offering views of the Sanctuary of St. Mary Magdalene and La Mola Castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is Picnic area near La Mola – La Mola summit loop from Paraje Natural Municipal Clots de la Sal y Monte de la Mola, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.1 km) path. This route leads to the summit of La Mola, providing expansive vistas of the Vinalopó Valley. Local hikers also love the Picnic area near La Mola – Casa Cueva loop from Paraje Natural Municipal Clots de la Sal y Monte de la Mola, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. This easy route explores the area around the Municipal Natural Park. Hiking around Novelda is defined by the Vinalopó Valley, rolling hills, and unique terracotta canyon landscapes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks through olive groves to more challenging ascents to viewpoints. The routes in Novelda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Novelda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Novelda?
A: Novelda offers a diverse network of over 20 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from easy strolls through natural parks to more challenging ascents with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Novelda?
A: Yes, Novelda has several easy routes perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5292551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic area near La Mola – Casa Cueva loop from Paraje Natural Municipal Clots de la Sal y Monte de la Mola&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.8-mile (6.1 km) trail that explores the area around the Municipal Natural Park in about 1 hour 52 minutes.

Q: What are some more challenging hiking options around Novelda?
A: For those seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31707445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic area near La Mola – La Mola summit loop from Novelda&lt;/a&gt; offer a moderate 7.8-mile (12.5 km) hike with rewarding views. The region also features higher mountainous areas like Alt del Bubo and Alt de la Casa Castalla, providing more strenuous climbs and 360-degree vistas.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Novelda?
A: Many of Novelda's trails are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e867422558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Novelda - La Mola (Circular PR-CV 311)&lt;/a&gt;, an 11 km route that takes you to La Mola and back, offering views of the Sanctuary of St. Mary Magdalene and La Mola Castle.

Q: Which hikes offer the best panoramic views of Novelda and the surrounding area?
A: Hikes ascending to La Mola, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/705241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic area near La Mola – La Mola summit loop from Paraje Natural Municipal Clots de la Sal y Monte de la Mola&lt;/a&gt;, provide stunning panoramic views of Novelda, the Vinalopó Valley, and the iconic Sanctuary of St. Mary Magdalene. Higher peaks like Alt del Bubo and Alt de la Casa Castalla also offer expansive 360-degree views.

Q: What unique natural features can I discover while hiking in Novelda?
A: Novelda is home to the unique terracotta canyon landscape of 'Les Salinetes de Novelda,' known for its clay formations. Within Les Salinetes, you can find the 'Clot del caño de agua dulce,' a hidden gem featuring a natural salt pool for floating and a mud bath area, along with an icy cold, drinkable water jet emerging from the mountain.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible by hiking in Novelda?
A: Absolutely. Many hikes lead to the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola summit&lt;/a&gt;, where you can visit the modernist &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of St. Mary Magdalene&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Both are perched at 360 meters above sea level, offering cultural exploration alongside your hike.

Q: What should I consider bringing for a hike in Novelda?
A: Given the region's climate, especially during warmer months, it's crucial to carry plenty of water. Many paths, particularly at higher elevations, offer limited shade. Sturdy shoes are recommended, and for more challenging routes, sure-footedness is beneficial.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Novelda?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from olive groves and vineyards to unique terracotta canyons, and the rewarding views from elevated points like La Mola.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Novelda?
A: Yes, many popular trailheads, especially those leading to La Mola and the Municipal Natural Park Clots de la Sal y Monte de la Mola, offer designated parking areas for hikers.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Novelda?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking in Novelda, offering milder temperatures. While winter can also be suitable, summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, and always with ample water.

Q: Are Novelda's hiking trails dog-friendly?
A: Most trails in Novelda are generally dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land. Remember to bring enough water for your pet, as natural water sources might be scarce on some routes.
tips: The church "Santuario de Santa María Magdalena" is considered a very unique and outstanding work within the Valencian modernism. It is no coincidence that it bears a certain resemblance to the Sagrada Familia Antonio Gaudís in Barcelona. José Sala Sala, the architect of the pilgrimage church, was clearly inspired by the world-famous building by Antonio Gaudí.
Beautiful views from the top of La Mola, you can appreciate the Castle of Novelda (or de la Mola)
